sources.writer_profile: Andy Hayler is one of the world's foremost professional restaurant critics, known for systematically reviewing three-Michelin-star establishments globally. He visited Noor in Córdoba in June 2024, shortly after it received its third Michelin star, providing a detailed independent critical assessment of chef Paco Morales's Al-Andalus tasting menu. His reviews are rigorously scored and consistently independent of commercial relationships.

Hanne Olsen is a Danish award-winning travel journalist who specialises in Spain, frequently living and working in the country. Her December 2025 guide to Córdoba's food scene reflects seven years of covering Andalucía's gastronomy, taberna culture, and the Montilla-Moriles DO, with a focus on authentic neighbourhood eating over tourist-facing venues.

Molly is a long-term British expat based in Spain since 1998, writing about Spanish food and travel under the name Piccavey. Her January 2026 Córdoba restaurant guide draws on repeated visits since 2006 and fluency in Spanish, prioritising local neighbourhood spots alongside tourist-facing tabernas. She also leads food tours in Andalucía.

Juan Velasco is a staff journalist at Cordópolis, the Córdoba edition of the national Spanish newspaper elDiario.es, covering culture, society and gastronomy in the city. His November 2025 article on Córdoba's Michelin star restaurants reports directly from the Michelin Guide awards ceremony, providing a local journalistic perspective on the city's fine-dining scene.

Alfonso Alba is a journalist at Cordópolis, the Córdoba local newspaper, covering gastronomy and society. His November 2025 article on the ReComiendo Michelin star award provides first-hand local reportage including chef Periko Ortega's own words and the historical background of the restaurant's trajectory since 2014.

sources.publication: El Debate / La Voz de Córdoba
sources.writer_profile: M. Ángeles Ramírez is a journalist with La Voz de Córdoba, the Córdoba section of the national digital newspaper El Debate, covering local gastronomy and society. Her articles from February 2026 and December 2025 represent consistent local food journalism focused on Córdoba's evolving restaurant scene, including coverage of the Repsol Guide additions and Michelin awards.

Brenda Alonso writes about travel and food for The Objective, an independent Spanish digital publication known for long-form journalism. Her August 2023 Córdoba restaurant guide covers thirteen establishments across the city's historic neighbourhoods, with a focus on traditional taberna culture and authentic local cuisine.

Txema Aguado is one half of the travel and gastronomy blog Yendo por la Vida, a Spanish-language platform covering food, sustainable tourism and cultural events across Spain. His January 2024 review of Choco offers a detailed first-person account of both tasting menus, including course-by-course descriptions and a kitchen visit with chef Kisko García.

Morgane Mazelier is a French travel writer who contributes to Andalucía Lovers, a bilingual travel blog focused on southern Spain. Her November 2024 Córdoba restaurant guide covers fifteen establishments across the city, with an emphasis on neighbourhood restaurants in Santa Marina and the Judería, based on personal visits.

restaurants.name: Noor
restaurants.signature_dish: Toasted pine nuts with green apple flavoured with ras al hanout and pine nut ice cream
restaurants.quote: Noor offers some very prettily presented and quite original food in a welcoming environment, and at a price that is not excessive.
restaurants.source_type_combination: EN primary + ES primary + EN primary + ES primary

restaurants.name: Choco
restaurants.signature_dish: Lechuga Aliñá; Gazpachuelo frío y Quisquillas; Cigala templada y morcilla
restaurants.quote: Recuerdos de su infancia en Villanueva de Córdoba y el Valle de Los Pedroches, impregnados de sal, vinagre y mantecas — a 17-course tasting menu drawing on Kisko García's childhood memories of the Pedroches Valley.
restaurants.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + EN primary

restaurants.name: ReComiendo
restaurants.signature_dish: Mazamorra; four appetizers themed around daily meals (breakfast, lunch, snack, dinner); artisanal breads with 20+ olive oils
restaurants.quote: Diversión, técnica, sabor, recuerdos, profesionalidad… y la personal filosofía culinaria del chef Periko Ortega, que hace todo lo posible por poner 'power' en los platos — Michelin Guide on awarding ReComiendo its first Michelin star in 2025.
restaurants.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + ES primary

restaurants.name: Casa Pepe de la Judería
name_variants: Restaurante Casa Pepe de la Judería
restaurants.signature_dish: Salmorejo cordobés; rabo de toro; honey roasted eggplant; fresh tuna
restaurants.quote: Pepe Giménez Aroca opened Casa Pepe in 1928 and today it's a Córdoba institution on a corner in the winding streets of the Jewish quarter, serving tapas and Andalusian dishes in both classic and modern versions.
restaurants.source_type_combination: EN primary + ES primary + EN primary + EN secondary

restaurants.name: La Taberna de Almodóvar
restaurants.cuisine: Traditional Córdoban taberna / Michelin Bib Gourmand
restaurants.signature_dish: Suckling lamb shoulder; croquetas with serrano ham; tuna tataki; berenjenas con miel
restaurants.quote: A Michelin Bib Gourmand taberna in the Santa Marina neighbourhood — suckling lamb shoulder and croquetas with serrano ham delivered with rich Córdoban stamp and outstanding value for money.
restaurants.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + EN secondary + EN primary

restaurants.name: Taberna La Montillana
restaurants.signature_dish: Oxtails in oloroso sauce; confit Iberian pork cheeks with foie gras; honey roasted eggplant; flamenquín
restaurants.quote: Both dishes — the oxtails in oloroso sauce and confit Iberian pork cheeks with foie gras — are some of the best versions I've had in Spain.
restaurants.source_type_combination: EN primary + EN primary + ES primary

restaurants.name: La Cuchara de San Lorenzo
name_variants: Restaurante La Cuchara de San Lorenzo
restaurants.cuisine: Traditional Córdoban with modern touch / Michelin Bib Gourmand
restaurants.signature_dish: Veal tripe; torrija caramelizada con sopa de coco; tapas and tasting menus with daily market specials
restaurants.quote: Run by two brothers — Narciso front of house and Paco in the kitchen — focusing on local and traditionally inspired cooking with lots of attention to detail and daily specials influenced by market availability; veal tripe among the best in the city.
restaurants.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + EN primary

restaurants.name: El Churrasco
restaurants.signature_dish: Churrasco cordobés — Iberian pork sirloin grilled over encina oak charcoal with two Arabic-influenced sauces; rabo de toro; jamón de bellota
restaurants.quote: Founded in 1970, El Churrasco has become a gastronomic institution in the heart of the Judería, celebrated for its meats grilled over encina oak charcoal and a wine cellar with over 900 references.
restaurants.source_type_combination: ES primary + EN primary + EN primary

restaurants.name: Regadera
restaurants.signature_dish: Ceviche de corvina; deshuesado rabo de toro con mousse de patata; creamy salmorejo with jamón serrano
restaurants.quote: Fresh and modern — chef Adrián Caballero's market-driven contemporary kitchen on the Ronda de Isasa with Michelin Bib Gourmand recognition and a visible herb garden.
restaurants.source_type_combination: ES primary + EN primary + ES primary

restaurants.name: Bar Santos
restaurants.signature_dish: Tortilla española — made with 6kg potatoes and 20 eggs, served steps from the Mezquita
restaurants.quote: Standing room only — this has become a must-do when visiting Córdoba for its legendary fluffy tortilla española with potato and onion.
restaurants.source_type_combination: EN primary + ES primary + EN primary

restaurants.name: Bodegas Campos
restaurants.signature_dish: Salmorejo tradicional; rabo de toro; bacalao confitado; deep ruby red gazpacho
restaurants.quote: A wine producer since 1908 — 'The deep ruby red colour of the gazpacho was like no other' — housed in a carefully restored bodega near Plaza del Potro, frequented by Spanish royalty and international figures who sign the Montilla-Moriles barrels.

restaurants.name: Amaltea
restaurants.signature_dish: Syrian pâté; muhammara (walnut and roasted pepper pâté); tabulé; dishes from ecological garden produce, certified meats and sustainably caught fish
restaurants.quote: A conscious and sustainable proposal on the banks of the Guadalquivir — cooking with produce from an ecological garden, certified meats and sustainably caught fish, newly recognised by the Repsol Guide 2026.
restaurants.source_type_combination: ES primary + EN primary + EN primary

restaurants.name: Taberna La Viuda
restaurants.signature_dish: Rabo de toro — winner, Best Rabo de Toro in Córdoba 2025 (Cofradía Gastronómica); lemon marinated salmon with saffron spiced couscous
restaurants.quote: The highlight was our rabo de toro, which was an intense explosion of flavor in La Viuda's version. After we visited the restaurant, La Viuda received the award for the best rabo de toro in Córdoba 2025.

restaurants.name: El Envero
restaurants.signature_dish: Olla podrida frita; royal de cocido con huevas y caviar; cordero al horno con wagyu A5; sorbete de piña con anís y mascarpone
restaurants.quote: Chef Manuel Valera's El Envero, in its ninth season, pays homage to the 18th century with a menu of gems: fried olla podrida, royal of stew with bottarga and caviar, and sea bass grilled with rose escabeche.

restaurants.name: Terra Olea
restaurants.cuisine: Contemporary Córdoban / organic / Michelin Bib Gourmand
restaurants.signature_dish: Vegetable-focused dishes with aromatic broths; recovery and adaptation of old Córdoban recipes through modern technique
restaurants.quote: Chef Paco Villar has based his proposal at Terra Olea on the recovery and adaptation of old recipes — a respectful reformulation of native Córdoban cuisine, awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand and one Repsol Sol.

restaurants.name: Taberna El Nº 10
restaurants.signature_dish: Salmorejo cordobés with jamón serrano and huevo duro; croquetas de cocido; selection of local fino and amontillado wines
restaurants.quote: Taberna El Nº 10 serves classic salmorejo cordobés with jamón serrano in a classic Judería setting — a neighbourhood taberna praised for its authentic Córdoban cooking and local wine selection.
restaurants.source_type_combination: ES primary + ES primary + EN primary

restaurants.name: Casa Rubio
restaurants.signature_dish: Flamenquines caseros; cordero sefardí; pastel cordobés; frituras
restaurants.quote: Casa Rubio blends tradition with modernity — serving flamenquines caseros and cordero sefardí with rooftop views of the historic quarter, steps from the Almódovar Gate in the Judería.

restaurants.name: Garum 2.1
restaurants.signature_dish: Salmorejo de mango con langostinos; octopus with fried pig ears over creamy potato purée; rabo de toro; torrija
restaurants.quote: An award-winning tapas bar at the foot of El Brillante offering modern reinterpretations of Andalucian classics — the octopus with fried pig ears over creamy potato is an unexpected Córdoba specialty.

restaurants.name: La Ermita de la Candelaria
restaurants.signature_dish: Pisto; mazamorra con aire de Almodóvar del Río; sorbete de naranja fresca con AOVE; tostas de steak tartar
restaurants.quote: Chef Javier Campos transformed a 15th-century hermitage into a Córdoban gastronomic reference — committed to la cocina de siempre with seasonal products from the mountains and orchards, awarded one Sol Repsol in 2024.

restaurants.name: La Casa de Manolete Bistró
restaurants.signature_dish: Beet salmorejo with tuna; creative mazamorra; iberian pork and game preparations
restaurants.quote: An elegant bistronomic restaurant housed in the reformed palace where bullfighter Manolete and philosopher Ortega y Gasset once lived — chefs Juanjo Ruiz and Reme Romero awarded one Sol Repsol.

restaurants.name: Los Berengueles
restaurants.signature_dish: Ensaladilla rusa (made fresh daily); salmorejo; rabo de toro; flamenquines; berenjenas con miel
restaurants.quote: A multi-generational family establishment near Calle Conde de Torres Cabrera emphasizing product quality — the ensaladilla rusa is made fresh daily and the rabo de toro is one of the stronger versions in the city.

restaurants.name: Salón de Té
restaurants.cuisine: Arabic / Andalusí tea room and full meals
restaurants.signature_dish: Tagine; couscous; ziryab eggplant; hummus; falafel; kibbeh; baklava; Andalusian and Arabic teas
restaurants.quote: Set in a courtyard decorated with colorful tiles, rugs and cushions — an oasis of calm in the centre of Córdoba, with homemade cakes, a large selection of Andalusian and Arabic teas, and a full menu of Arabic and Andalusian dishes.

restaurants.name: El Astronauta
restaurants.signature_dish: Moussaka vegana; salmorejo con aguacate; healthy midweek menu del día at €10
restaurants.quote: A cool restaurant below the Roman Temple with healthy options and vegetarian dishes — the €10 midweek menu makes it a local favourite for affordable creative eating in the historic centre.

restaurants.name: La Boca
restaurants.signature_dish: Three-course vegetarian daily menu; zero-kilometre products; wood-fired preparations with Japanese-influenced elements
restaurants.quote: A welcoming café and restaurant on Calle San Fernando offering a three-course vegetarian daily menu alongside meat options, with loyal customers for over a decade.

restaurants.name: Taberna La Cazuela de la Espartería
restaurants.signature_dish: Flamenquín cordobés (fried sirloin rolled in serrano ham); salmorejo; stuffed mussels; small fried fish; tuna tartare
restaurants.quote: Since the restaurant opened in 1998, the focus has been on local gastronomy — festively decorated in Córdoba blue and white, serving flamenquín cordobés, salmorejo, and small fried fish near Plaza Corredera.
restaurants.geo_matched_name: Taberna La Cazuela de la Esparteria

restaurants.name: La Taberna del Río
restaurants.signature_dish: Salmorejo; local cheeses from Pedroches Valley; Montilla-Moriles wines
restaurants.quote: Highly recommended for typical local Córdoban delicacies — salmorejo, local Pedroches cheese, and Montilla-Moriles wine served on the Paseo de la Ribera alongside the Guadalquivir.

restaurants.name: Bodegas Mezquita Céspedes
restaurants.cuisine: Traditional Córdoban / tapas and menú del día
restaurants.signature_dish: Remojón cordobés; bacalao confitado en AOVE con pisto cordobés; tajín de cordero sefardí; rabo de toro a la cordobesa; carrillera ibérica
restaurants.quote: Named Spain's best casual restaurant in 2024 — a menú del día at €20.60 featuring remojón cordobés, bacalao confitado en AOVE con pisto cordobés, and rabo de toro a la cordobesa.

restaurants.name: Casa Salvador-Dmerkado
restaurants.signature_dish: Rabo de toro; tartares (tuna and steak); rice dishes; seasonal vegetables with local sierra meat
restaurants.quote: Un escenario de elegancia sobria donde la calidad del producto y la tranquilidad del entorno garantizan una experiencia impecable — a setting of sober elegance where quality produce guarantees a flawless experience, newly recognised by Repsol 2026.

restaurants.name: Taberna San Miguel Casa El Pisto
restaurants.signature_dish: Pisto con huevo frito; pescaito frito; iberian ham; traditional stews
restaurants.quote: Operating since 1880, this authentic tavern on Plaza de San Miguel serves pisto con huevo frito and pescaito frito at very moderate prices in classic Córdoban décor.
restaurants.geo_matched_name: Casa El Pisto - Taberna San Miguel

restaurants.name: La Tinaja
restaurants.signature_dish: Aubergines with honey; potato pots with brava sauce; cod-filled ravioli
restaurants.quote: Service here was excellent and the atmosphere inside was relaxed and welcoming — a riverside restaurant offering three courses for around €28, with aubergines with honey and cod-filled ravioli among the highlights.

restaurants.name: La Maceta
restaurants.signature_dish: Argentinian burger with chimichurri and crispy onions; ensalada russa; sirloin with tomato, mushrooms and caramelized onions
restaurants.quote: A small, bright and modern tapas bar on Plaza del Escudo with over 40 tapas on the menu, including both classic Córdoban and contemporary flavors.

restaurants.name: Taberna El Abanico
restaurants.signature_dish: Berenjenas con miel de caña (eggplant with cane honey)
restaurants.quote: Located on Calle Velázquez Bosco near Plaza de San Miguel, Taberna El Abanico is celebrated for its berenjenas con miel de caña — fried eggplant drizzled with cane honey, the definitive Córdoban signature dish.

restaurants.name: Taberna Góngora
restaurants.signature_dish: Flamenquines de Córdoba; salmorejo; grilled octopus; croquetas
restaurants.quote: Located near the Mosque-Cathedral on Calle Conde de Torres Cabrera, Taberna Góngora serves classic flamenquines de Córdoba and salmorejo at affordable prices in an unpretentious neighbourhood setting.

restaurants.name: Mercado Victoria
restaurants.signature_dish: Galician octopus; salmorejo; black-footed Córdoba ham; ice cream La Mezquita; paella; bastela
restaurants.quote: In the middle of Córdoba's green lung, the Jardínes de la Victoria, lies Córdoba's modern gastronomy market — over 20 different stalls with typical tapas from Córdoba and other parts of the world.
restaurants.flag_note: rec-remove flag overridden by user: accepted as gastromarket venue

restaurants.name: La Lianta de Quini
restaurants.signature_dish: Almond mazamorra with apple and sardines; oxtail canelones
restaurants.quote: A creative contemporary taberna in the Judería neighbourhood serving almond mazamorra with apple and sardines and oxtail canelones — inventive updates on Córdoban classics.
